skill.name: zoho-crm-automation
skill.description: Automate Zoho CRM tasks through Rube MCP and Composio, including searches, record creation, updates, tags, related records, and lead conversion.
skill.summary: Guide AI assistants through safe Zoho CRM automation using current Rube MCP tool schemas.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks, tool names, and CRM text. The confirmed risk is legitimate network-based CRM automation through Rube MCP, which can read and modify sensitive Zoho CRM records.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Access to CRM Data
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to connect Rube MCP at an external URL and use it with Zoho CRM. This is expected behavior, but it can expose CRM data to a third-party integration path and should require user-approved OAuth access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 20 names the external MCP endpoint, and lines 23-25 instruct connection management and OAuth completion. This confirms network integration, but it appears intentional rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: CRM Record Modification Capabilities
medium_findings.description: The skill provides workflows for creating, updating, converting, tagging, and linking Zoho CRM records. These operations are legitimate for CRM automation but can change business data if used without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences directly include create, update, convert, and related-record operations. The risk is data integrity impact, not hidden code execution.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick hits refer to inline code formatting around MCP and Zoho tool names, parameters, and examples. No evidence found of shell execution, script files, or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are markdown documentation and parameter examples. They do not contain executable shell syntax or runtime code.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to the skill description, and the reconnaissance finding points to record ID guidance. No evidence found of cryptographic code, host inspection, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text about Zoho CRM automation, and line 88 discusses Zoho record IDs. The static labels do not match the semantic content.

content.value_statement: Manual CRM updates slow sales and operations teams. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Zoho CRM tasks with current Rube MCP schemas.
actual_capabilities: Search Zoho CRM records by module, field, and criteria.
Create leads, contacts, deals, accounts, and other supported module records.
Update existing records after finding the correct Zoho record ID.
Convert leads into contacts, accounts, and optional deals.
Create tags and update related records across supported modules.
Guide schema discovery before actions by using Rube MCP tool search.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and Zoho OAuth authorization.
Does not include executable scripts or direct Zoho API client code.
Bulk changes can hit Zoho plan limits and should be rate limited.
Lead conversion is irreversible in the documented workflow.

use_cases.title: Qualify inbound leads
use_cases.description: Search for existing records, create missing leads, and tag them for sales follow-up.

use_cases.title: Maintain clean CRM records
use_cases.description: Find contacts or accounts, update changed fields, and preserve unchanged Zoho data.

use_cases.title: Convert sales-ready leads
use_cases.description: Locate a lead, confirm conversion details, and create the related contact, account, or deal.

prompt_templates.title: Find a CRM record
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zoho CRM automation skill to search for a contact by email. Confirm the module fields first, then show the matching record summary.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Zoho CRM lead after checking the required Lead fields. Ask me for any missing required values before creating the record.

prompt_templates.title: Update an existing contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the contact by email, confirm the record ID, and update only the phone number and job title I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a qualified lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the lead, summarize the conversion impact, ask for confirmation, then convert it into a contact and deal with the provided deal details.

output_examples.output: The assistant checks available modules and fields, searches Contacts by email, and returns the matching contact summary with the Zoho record ID.

output_examples.input: Create a new lead for Acme with this email and phone number.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies required Lead fields, asks for any missing required values, and creates the lead after confirmation.

output_examples.input: Convert this qualified lead and open a deal.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the lead, explains that conversion is irreversible, confirms account and deal details, and then runs the conversion.

best_practices: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before every workflow.
Confirm record IDs and changed fields before writing to Zoho CRM.
Use pagination and respect Zoho rate limits for large record sets.
anti_patterns: Do not convert leads without explicit user confirmation.
Do not update records using display labels instead of API field names.
Do not run broad record retrieval when a targeted search can answer the request.

faq.question: Does this skill require Zoho API keys?
faq.answer: No. It uses Rube MCP and a Zoho OAuth connection managed through the Rube connection flow.

faq.question: Can it create and update CRM records?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents create, update, tag, related-record, and lead conversion workflows for supported Zoho modules.

faq.question: Does it run local scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation for MCP tool usage and does not include local executable scripts.

faq.question: Why should schemas be checked first?
faq.answer: Zoho fields and MCP tool schemas can vary. Checking schemas helps avoid missing required fields or using wrong field names.

faq.question: Is lead conversion reversible?
faq.answer: No. The skill notes that lead conversion removes the original lead from the Leads module.

faq.question: What permissions are needed?
faq.answer: The connected Zoho user needs access to the target modules and operations in their CRM role and profile.
